Endpoint: Symptom reduction — loss of diagnosis; score below clinical threshold
Key sources: de Jongh 2024 (88% CPTSD remission, 8 days); Sciarrino 2020 (d=1.57, 1–3 weeks); Mitchell 2021 (67% PTSD loss, 18 weeks); Feder 2025 (d=1.9, 3 weeks)
Example measures: CAPS-5, PCL-5, ITQ, BDI, CORE-OM below clinical threshold

Endpoint: Functional improvement — holding employment; maintaining relationships; reduced crises
Key sources: Links 2015 (GPM milestones: 6 wks → 18 mo); Fonagy 2015 (TADS functional gains at 42-month follow-up); Bateman & Fonagy 2008 (vocational improvement at 8-year follow-up)
Example measures: Employment status, crisis frequency, GAF, social functioning scales

Endpoint: Structural change — personality reorganisation, mentalizing capacity, integration of dissociated parts, earned attachment security
Evidence type: Long-term follow-up studies, case studies, naturalistic data
Key sources: Lindfors 2019 (Helsinki 10-year); Leichsenring 2013 (Perry: 50% PD recovery at 92 sessions, 75% at 216); Griffiths 2025 (DID: 3–5 years); Murphy 2009 (160 sessions / 5 years); Heacock 2022 (15 years weekly)
Example measures: LPO, SCL-90, defence style, AAI classification, MID scores

Endpoint: Growth and reconnection — PTG, identity transformation, capacity for intimacy and agency
Key sources: Foo 2022 (10+ years); Walker 2013 (40+ years); Eger 2017 (healing ongoing at 90+); Joseph 2012 (PTG continuing indefinitely); reddit-2024-recovered-cptsd (5 years to recovered)
Example measures: PTG-I, narrative coherence, identity integration, relational capacity (no standard instrument)

Endpoint: Recovery as direction — heading north; functional markers of directional progress, no completion point
Key sources: Spring 2020 (heading north metaphor: 15-year arc from DID to undeniably good life)
Example measures: Living mostly in green zone, remembering without reliving, choosing from front brain, experiencing joy alongside grief
